# UC-22.13.48 · BES Cyber System Restore Drill — RTO Measurement

## Description

We compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes apps, itsm and sourcetypes http:ping`, restore_drill, restore_drill`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Populate RTO targets from recovery plans. (2) Validate timestamps from orchestration tools. (3) Track remediation for misses. (4) Annual leadership briefing export. Indexes required: `index=apps`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=http:ping``, `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `sourcetype=restore_drill``; Lookups: `cip009_rto_targets`, `cip009_rto_targets.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `index=apps` `sourcetype=http:ping`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate RTO targets from recovery plans. (2) Validate timestamps from orchestration tools. (3) Track remediation for misses. (4) Annual leadership briefing export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill bes_system=* earliest=-400d
| eval start=strptime(started_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=strptime(restored_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rto_min=round((end-start)/60,2)
| lookup cip009_rto_targets.csv bes_system OUTPUT target_min
| where rto_min > target_min
| table drill_id, bes_system, rto_min, target_min, restored_at
```

Understanding this SPL

**BES Cyber System Restore Drill — RTO Measurement (CIP-009-6 R5 Part 5.1)** — We compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `index=apps` `sourcetype=http:ping`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: restore_drill. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=restore_drill, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **start** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **end** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rto_min** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where rto_min > target_min` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: bes_system, drill_id, end, restored_at, rto_min, start, target_min.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the official BES or cyber-asset list, the ESP edge firewall, and the same Windows or network data this search is built to mirror for that time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart (RTO vs target), Table (misses), Single value (worst RTO)

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill bes_system=* earliest=-400d
| eval start=strptime(started_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=strptime(restored_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rto_min=round((end-start)/60,2)
| lookup cip009_rto_targets.csv bes_system OUTPUT target_min
| where rto_min > target_min
| table drill_id, bes_system, rto_min, target_min, restored_at
```

## Visualization

Column chart (RTO vs target), Table (misses), Single value (worst RTO). Key fields: `bes_system`, `drill_id`, `end`, `restored_at`, `rto_min`, `start`.
